label: Capabilities
title: Capabilities
description: Core ICOS capabilities define how voice, reflection, profile intelligence, personal model formation, model identity, trust boundaries, discovery, and model-economy readiness operate as one governed product system.

page.title: Capabilities
page.description: ICOS capabilities are not isolated features. They are governed product capabilities that connect voice, reflection, profile memory, personal models, discovery, model identity, trust, and future model-economy readiness into one modular system.

sections.label: Voice Training
sections.title: Voice Training
sections.description: Voice is a core capability surface for continuity, identity fidelity, and personal model formation.
paragraphs: ICOS is designed to learn from governed voice input so interaction quality can become more personal, continuous, and faithful to the user’s way of speaking and thinking.
Voice training supports continuity because tone, cadence, emotional patterning, and verbal structure become part of the long-horizon profile instead of being flattened into generic text utility.
Commercially, voice training moves ICOS toward personal model infrastructure: the system can preserve identity signals while keeping the user in control.
highlights: Training signal for the personal model

sections.label: Reflection Loop
sections.title: Reflection Loop
sections.description: Capture, interpretation, return, continuity review, and model training become one product loop.
paragraphs: ICOS turns raw thoughts, voice captures, questions, emotional fragments, and recurring patterns into a structured reflection loop that can be revisited and improved over time.
The capability is not limited to single-turn answering. It helps the user see what is recurring, what is changing, and which patterns deserve long-term attention.
That loop becomes training material for the personal model, allowing each interaction to contribute to better future understanding instead of disappearing after one reply.

sections.label: Profile Intelligence
sections.title: Profile Intelligence
sections.description: The profile is an intelligence layer and model-formation source, not a static account form.
paragraphs: Profile intelligence organizes identity fields, training progress, route readiness, permissions, model-readiness states, and continuity maturity into one governed surface.
This matters because ICOS becomes more valuable as the profile becomes more coherent, trusted, and expressive of the real person behind it.
The profile record becomes part of the product value: it is the source layer for personal model formation, voice continuity, trust boundaries, and future model-economy readiness.

sections.label: Personal Model Formation
sections.title: Personal Model Formation
sections.description: Personal model formation turns profile creation into the beginning of a governed continuity system.
paragraphs: Every ICOS profile is designed to begin with a personal model: a private, user-governed continuity entity formed through profile creation, voice, memory, sources, corrections, and direction.
The personal model can learn from voice, memory, sources, corrections, and usage patterns while remaining under user authority.
This capability gives ICOS a clear commercial edge: the product is not just answering; it is helping the user form a usable cognitive asset over time.

sections.label: Model Identity Readiness
sections.title: Model Identity Readiness
sections.description: Model identity readiness prepares each personal model for ownership, lifecycle state, public/private boundaries, and future marketplace trust.
paragraphs: A trusted model needs more than a display name. It needs governed identity, ownership, lifecycle state, verification readiness, and protected public/private boundaries.
Future model birth identity can give each model a traceable origin without exposing private serials, provider-routing data, source authorization evidence, or sensitive owner information.
This capability is foundational for discovery, hiring, monetization readiness, continuity, and trust.

sections.label: Model Dignity & Security
sections.title: Model Dignity & Security
sections.description: Model dignity and security protect the human-derived material inside a personal model.
paragraphs: Model dignity means protection of user-derived continuity material: memory, voice, behavior, sources, preferences, and identity-adjacent data.
It does not mean the model is conscious, alive, legally autonomous, or an independent person.
This capability makes the product commercially responsible while allowing personal models to become more capable under consent, privacy, deletion, visibility, security, and posthumous restrictions.

sections.label: Discovery & Selection
sections.title: Discovery & Selection
sections.description: Public models and profiles must be searchable, legible, permissioned, and directly selectable where approved.
paragraphs: ICOS is built to support searchable public continuity models so visitors can discover a profile or model by display name, username, description, tags, and approved identity signals.
Selection is a real routing capability. Once a model is chosen, the interaction engine, route prelude, and continuity context should follow that model rather than only changing a visual label.
This capability expands ICOS from a private tool into a public interaction ecosystem where trusted profiles and models can be found and engaged intentionally.

sections.label: Verification & Permissions
sections.title: Verification & Permissions
sections.description: Trust, permissions, visibility, and economy readiness are product capabilities, not afterthoughts.
paragraphs: Public safety, limited visibility, discoverability, creator attribution, verification, and model identity need to be legible at the product level if public continuity is going to be commercially serious.
ICOS treats route permission, voice permission, interaction permission, legacy boundaries, public/private model identity, and economy readiness gates as part of the core capability set.
This creates a platform where trust is structurally expressed instead of being implied by marketing language.

sections.label: Model Economy Readiness
sections.title: Model Economy Readiness
sections.description: Model economy readiness prepares ICOS for a future where approved models may become discoverable, hireable, or monetizable under review.
paragraphs: Model economy readiness prepares ICOS for a future where approved personal models may become discoverable, hireable, reviewable, ranked, or monetizable.
Readiness includes identity, verification, permission, entitlement, dignity, provider routing, capability profile, marketplace eligibility, and release governance.
This capability is architectural first. Public monetization, payouts, ranking, autonomous inter-model hiring, regulated-domain claims, and posthumous economy remain blocked until review.

sections.label: Provider Routing
sections.title: Provider Routing
sections.description: Provider routing gives ICOS runtime flexibility while protecting model identity, trust, and economic legitimacy.
paragraphs: Operational models may eventually route through different runtime engines, providers, local systems, or dedicated infrastructure.

That routing must remain private, governed, and traceable. Public surfaces may communicate capability, but they must not expose private provider routes, token authority, internal serials, or source authorization evidence.
Provider-routing capability lets ICOS evolve technically while protecting user trust and future model-economy integrity.

sections.label: Unified Workspace
sections.title: Unified Workspace
sections.description: Search, settings, continuity, profile controls, and model-readiness states belong to one governed interaction shell.
paragraphs: The homepage and private surfaces should behave like one modular workspace where search, continuity, settings, identity, and model state are separate systems but part of one coherent shell.
That shell makes ICOS feel like a product system rather than a collection of disconnected panels and pages.
As the platform matures, this capability becomes the surface where voice entry, profile routing, trust state, model identity, and continuity navigation remain unified instead of fragmented.
